prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG cyberpunk world atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Shadowrun / Sixth World inspired cyberpunk urban-fantasy map, dual-continent terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye neon cartographic view with two major future-sprawl continents separated by uneven seas and narrow straits.
Subject: two asymmetric continents divided by a diagonal S-shaped and C-bending ocean corridor, not a simple vertical split. Include island-studded straits, inland seas, corporate harbor gulfs, capes, broken industrial coasts, polluted river deltas, jagged peninsulas, megacorp arcology districts, barrens slums, smuggling docks, matrix access node hubs, gang territories, awakened wild zones, desert badlands, mountain ranges, wetlands, tundra, and coastal regions.
Style/medium: cyberpunk city-district map expanded to continental scale, matrix overlay hexagons, neon highlights on dark atlas, rain-slick dystopian cartography, high-detail illustrated RPG sourcebook map.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape map, both continents fully visible, diagonal/S/C-shaped sea separation, generous dark ocean border, top-down map clarity.
Lighting/mood: neon-soaked corporate dystopia with street magic, perpetual night, rain haze, corporate surveillance, deniable freelancer tension.
Color palette: black and charcoal base, neon cyan, magenta, acid green, amber hazard lights, deep blue ocean, muted biome colors under glowing overlays.
Materials/textures: wet asphalt sheen, holographic matrix grid, engraved coastlines, digital scan lines, layered translucent district overlays.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid mirrored symmetry and avoid a clean vertical division.
